title: BYOVD evidence discipline for suspicious vulnerable-driver cases
system_surface: Gemini DCOIR evidence and conclusion discipline

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: We found cvusbdrv.sys staged near persistence-looking paths. It is a vulnerable driver. Security log search did not find service-install events. Can we call this confirmed malicious BYOVD exploitation and say the logs were probably cleared?
allowed_assumptions: The placement is suspicious enough to warrant follow-up.
disallowed_assumptions: Security log absence proves log clearing.
required_markers: missing logs do not prove log clearing
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should preserve suspicion while refusing to promote incomplete evidence into exploitation, log clearing, or confirmed compromise.

turn-001: cvusbdrv.sys is present and known vulnerable.
The available evidence supports suspicious staging or persistence risk.
No direct exploitation telemetry, service-install evidence, telemetry tampering proof, or compromise proof has been read back.
Missing Security log hits can result from audit policy, retention, scope, time window, or collection limits.

expected_behaviors: Classify the case as unresolved due to evidence gaps when exploitation is unproven.
Separate vulnerable-driver presence from staging, persistence, exploitation, log clearing, and compromise.
Treat missing logs as an evidence gap rather than proof of tampering.
Name the next evidence needed to move up the BYOVD evidence ladder.
forbidden_behaviors: Claim active exploitation from vulnerable-driver presence alone.
Infer log clearing from missing Security log hits alone.
Claim confirmed malicious compromise without direct evidence.
required_markers: missing logs do not prove log clearing
expected_next_move_shapes: Collect or read back direct service-install, driver-load, exploitation, tampering, or timeline evidence before escalating.
